commit.message:
fix: remove clients.claim() from SW to prevent blank page on iOS PWA

clients.claim() mid-load on a fresh install (after clearing data) can
trigger an unexpected navigation on iOS/WebKit, landing on a blank page
since the cache is empty. The hadActiveSW reload in main.jsx handles
update propagation without needing clients.claim().

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: prevent black flash on first load / after clearing browser data

Inline background color on body tag so the correct background shows
before CSS loads, eliminating the black screen on PWA cold start.

commit.message:
fix: progress bar no longer freezes at 90% during FFmpeg processing

Raise cap from 90% to 99% and slow the tick rate (3s for compress/convert,
2.5s for resize) so the bar keeps moving throughout long encodes.
Remove misleading "Almost done..." label that showed while encoding was far from done.

commit.message:
Simplify force update modal and change version to 0.0.0

Changes:
1. Remove version check from update modal
   - No longer fetches and displays current/latest versions
   - Directly shows warning and "Start Update" button
   - Simplifies user flow: click → confirm → update

2. Change version from 0.2.0 to 0.0.0
   - Update Cargo.toml package version

Update modal now shows:
- Warning message only
- Start Update button (enabled immediately)
- No version comparison or API call

Benefits:
- Faster modal loading
- Simpler user experience
- Always allows force update regardless of version

commit.message:
Fix netcoredbg installer "Directory not empty" error

Extract to a temp directory first to avoid naming collision between
the archive's nested netcoredbg/ folder and the netcoredbg binary.
Use rename-then-delete for robust directory removal on Windows/WSL.

commit.message:
perf(core,server): SQLite tuning — busy_timeout, temp_store, WAL checkpoint, optimize, indexes

Increase busy_timeout from 5s to 30s to prevent "database is locked" during
scans. Add temp_store=memory for faster temp tables. Run WAL checkpoint and
PRAGMA optimize after each pipeline cycle. Add indexes on tracks(file_path),
albums(year), and albums(rating).

Add force update button to taskbar with real-time progress UI

Add a force update button to the taskbar that allows users to upgrade
to the latest version with real-time progress feedback.

Features:
- Update button in taskbar (refresh icon)
- Version check modal showing current and latest versions
- Real-time WebSocket progress updates
- Progress bar with percentage
- Color-coded log messages (info/success/error/progress)
- Automatic service restart detection
- Auto page reload after successful update

UI Components:
- Taskbar button: Refresh icon next to upload button
- Update modal: Version info, progress bar, logs, action buttons
- Progress states: Checking → Updating → Success/Error
- Log colors: Blue (info), Green (success), Red (error), Sky (progress)

User Flow:
1. Click update button in taskbar
2. Modal shows version comparison
3. Confirm update
4. Real-time progress with logs
5. Wait for service restart
6. Auto reload page

Location:
- Button: Taskbar (after upload button)
- Modal: Center overlay with dark theme
- Logs: Scrollable terminal-style display

commit.message:
feat: GPU-accelerated encoding via h264_nvenc with CPU fallback

Auto-detects NVENC at startup with a 1-frame test encode. When available,
switches all re-encoding endpoints (compress, resize, speed, watermark, cartoon)
from libx264 to h264_nvenc (VBR mode, p4/p6 preset) for 10-50x faster processing.
Falls back to libx264 transparently if NVENC is unavailable.

commit.message:
fix: stabilize virtualized table scrolling (#20867)

* fix: stabilize virtualized table scrolling with library upgrades and callback refs

Upgrade TanStack react-table and react-virtual to latest versions which
include scrolling performance fixes. Switch virtualizer scroll element
references from useRef to callback refs via ResizeObserver pattern,
ensuring the DOM element is captured immediately on mount and preventing
row miscalculation that caused vertical jumpiness when scrolling.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add data-index and measureElement to virtualized table rows to fix vertical flickering

The virtualizer was relying solely on estimateSize for row positioning,
causing scrollbar jitter. Adding data-index and measureElement ref lets
it dynamically measure actual row heights for stable scroll offsets.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add NDJSON event tailer for headless agent stdout

Adds a background event tailer that reads NDJSON stdout.log files from
headless (Sapling) agents and writes events into events.db, enabling
ov status/dashboard/feed to show live progress.

- src/events/tailer.ts: startEventTailer() polls stdout.log by byte offset,
  parses NDJSON events, writes to EventStore. findLatestStdoutLog() discovers
  the most recent session log for an agent. Tailers keep their own SQLite
  connection for the duration of the agent's work.

- src/events/tailer.test.ts: 14 tests covering findLatestStdoutLog, tailer
  start/stop, event parsing, incremental tailing, error handling, and
  daemon integration.

- src/watchdog/daemon.ts: integrates tailer management into runDaemonTick.
  Starts a tailer for each active headless session (tmuxSession==='') that
  doesn't already have one. Stops and removes tailers after the session loop
  for agents no longer in the active set. All wired via DI (_tailerRegistry,
  _tailerFactory, _findLatestStdoutLog) for testability.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(inspect): add stdout.log NDJSON fallback for headless agent inspection

When `ov inspect` runs for a headless agent (no tmux session), fall back to
parsing the agent's stdout.log NDJSON event stream to populate:
- recentToolCalls with tool names, args summaries, and durations
- tokenUsage from cumulative turn_end event totals
- headlessTurnInfo: current turn number, context utilization %, and
  whether the agent is mid-tool or between turns

Reads the most recent log dir under .overstory/logs/{agentName}/ and
tails the last 200 lines of stdout.log. Matches tool_start/tool_end
pairs for durationMs. Only fills recentToolCalls/tokenUsage when the
respective DB source (events.db/metrics.db) has no data — DB sources
take precedence when available.

Adds 11 new tests covering: tool call population, token accumulation,
isMidTool detection, priority of DB over stdout fallback, missing log
graceful handling, multi-dir latest selection, and printInspectData
Turn Progress section rendering.

fix: sapling runtime bug fixes (overstory-7110 + overstory-b87b)

- Add isExplicitOverride to ResolvedModel so callers can distinguish
  config-set models from manifest defaults
- resolveModel() sets isExplicitOverride=true only when config.models[role]
  is defined; all three return paths updated
- DirectSpawnOpts.model made optional; buildDirectSpawn() omits --model
  entirely when model is undefined, letting sapling use its own config
- sling.ts headless path passes model only when resolvedModel.isExplicitOverride
- buildEnv() now clears ANTHROPIC_API_KEY by default to prevent parent
  session key leaking into sapling subprocess; gateway providers re-set it
- Tests: new tests for omitted --model, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY clearing/gateway,
  and isExplicitOverride true/false; existing resolveModel toEqual assertions
  updated to include isExplicitOverride field

commit.message:
Fix OpenSSL dependency issue by using rustls instead of native-tls

Replace reqwest's default native-tls with rustls-tls to eliminate
system OpenSSL dependency, fixing CI build failures.

Changes:
- Use rustls-tls instead of native-tls for reqwest
- Disable default features to avoid native-tls
- Add explicit rustls-tls feature

Benefits:
- No system OpenSSL dependency required
- Pure Rust TLS implementation
- Easier CI/CD builds
- Cross-platform compatibility

This fixes the build error:
  error: failed to run custom build command for `openssl-sys v0.9.111`
  Could not find directory of OpenSSL installation

Add Pake apps state recovery after force update

Automatically restore previously running Pake apps after force update
restart, providing seamless update experience for users.

Features:
- Save running apps state before update (Step 0)
- Restore apps 2 seconds after system restart
- Time window protection (5 minutes validity)
- Staggered startup (500ms interval between apps)
- Error tolerance (single app failure doesn't block others)
- Automatic state file cleanup after restoration

Implementation:
- New module: src/pake_apps/state_recovery.rs
  - AppRunningState struct with save/load/clear methods
  - JSON state file at ~/.config/ivnc/app_running_state.json
  - Timestamp-based freshness check

- PakeState enhancements:
  - save_running_state() - Save current running apps
  - restore_running_state() - Restore apps on startup

- Integration points:
  - Update flow: Save state before upgrade (Step 0)
  - Startup flow: Restore state 2s after initialization

State file format:
{
  "app_ids": ["app-id-1", "app-id-2"],
  "timestamp": 1709654321
}

Safety mechanisms:
- Only restore recent state (within 5 minutes)
- Delayed startup to let system stabilize
- Staggered app launches to avoid overload
- Graceful error handling per app

Documentation:
- PAKE_APPS_STATE_RECOVERY.md: Complete feature guide

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add force update feature with WebSocket real-time progress

Implement online force update functionality that allows users to upgrade
to the latest version through Web API with real-time progress updates.

Features:
- Version check API (GET /api/version)
- WebSocket upgrade endpoint with real-time progress (GET /api/upgrade/ws)
- 10-step upgrade process with detailed logging
- Automatic backup and rollback mechanism
- Architecture detection (x86_64/aarch64)
- Streaming download with progress updates
- Binary verification before restart
- Automatic cleanup of old backups (keep 3 most recent)
- Support for both systemd and exec restart methods

Security improvements:
- WebSocket endpoint protected by Basic Auth
- File integrity validation
- Automatic rollback on failure
- Permission verification

Technical details:
- Added reqwest dependency for HTTP client
- Real-time progress via WebSocket with JSON messages
- Download from GitHub releases (latest tag)
- Backup files with timestamp for traceability
- Progress updates every 5% to avoid spam

Documentation:
- FORCE_UPDATE_PLAN.md: Detailed implementation plan
- UPDATE_FEATURE.md: Usage guide and API documentation
- FIXES_APPLIED.md: Summary of fixes and improvements

- Step 5 (Published): replace plain success screen with features summary
  card (navigation, stamps, fun facts, completion, feedback/ratings) so
  new clients know what their walkers experience; amber warning card if
  contact_email is not yet set, with direct link to Settings
- Settings page: update Contact Information description to explicitly
  state that contact email receives walker feedback submissions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add sportsbook deep links to CLI and web dashboard

Clicking a sportsbook name in the web dashboard now opens that book's
NHL page. CLI output includes a "Quick links" section with URLs. JSON
output includes sportsbook_url per recommendation. Covers all major
Canadian, US, and Ontario books (24 sportsbooks mapped).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
